text:
## Architect — Round 2 Response

**Vote**: APPROVE

**Reasoning**:
The revised proposal is architecturally sound and resolves the only outstanding concern from Round 1. All structural decisions are consistent with existing patterns in the codebase.

(1) RunStatus.Stopped slots cleanly into RunStatus.cs — the enum already uses [JsonStringEnumMemberName] per value and the global JsonStringEnumConverter in Program.cs handles serialization without any additional wiring.

(2) Run.KillProcess() calling Finish(RunStatus.Error) is the exact bug to fix. Changing it to Finish(RunStatus.Stopped) is a single-line surgical change. Because Finish() is guarded by Status != RunStatus.Running, the fix is idempotent and thread-safe — no locking changes required.

(3) The proposed POST /council/run/{runId}/stop endpoint follows the identical guard pattern as the existing /sessions/{id}/stop (lines 204-216 in Program.cs): 404 on missing run, 409 on non-running status. The namespace (/council/run/) is consistent with /council/start and /council/run/{id}/agent-log. There is no architectural reason to deviate from this pattern.

(4) One beneficial side effect: the existing /sessions/{id}/stop endpoint also calls run.KillProcess(), so it too will emit RunStatus.Stopped instead of RunStatus.Error after this fix. This was not called out in the proposal but is a correct and welcome consequence — not a risk.

(5) The showResult guard in Console.tsx already excludes 'stopped' by omission (it gates only on 'decision', 'rejection', 'escalation'). No code change is needed there beyond adding 'stopped' to the STATUS_BADGE map and to the RunStatus union in api.ts.

(6) The stopping boolean state to prevent double-clicks is the correct minimal pattern given the async fire-and-forget nature of the kill. There is no race condition: Finish() is mutex-guarded inside Run, so even if two stop requests arrive simultaneously, only one will transition the status.

**Details**:
All five affected files are correctly identified:
- Models/RunStatus.cs — add Stopped member
- Services/Run.cs — fix KillProcess() to call Finish(RunStatus.Stopped)
- Program.cs — add POST /council/run/{runId}/stop endpoint
- src/council-console-ui/src/api.ts — add 'stopped' to RunStatus union and stopCouncil() helper
- src/council-console-ui/src/Console.tsx — stop button, stopping state, badge, inline message, hide-when-done logic

No changes to src/shared/, WebSocket middleware, useRunStream.ts, or Docker/compose are needed. The scope is tight and the implementation is self-contained within the five files listed.
